commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f12c034a249f84d26a298dfa38653938df9c9bc7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the QMD memory manager to route glob-pattern collections through native recursive fs.watch on macOS/Windows with chokidar fallback, plus unit tests for routing, fallback, close, and reactivity.

PR surface: Source +173, Tests +398. Total +571 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. live macOS QMD FD repro was run in this read-only review. The source path is clear: current main still sends QMD glob paths to chokidar, and the linked merged sibling fix proves the same watcher class for memory directories.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The external PR needs contributor-owned real behavior proof plus maintainer review of the watcher parity repair; ClawSweeper should not take over while the proof gate is still insufficient.

target.title: fix(memory-core): use native recursive fs.watch in QMD watcher to prevent per-file FD leak
target.body:
## Summary

Fix the QMD backend's `ensureWatcher()` to use native recursive `fs.watch(dir, { recursive: true })` for directory collections instead of chokidar with glob patterns like `**/*.md`. This prevents the per-file FSEvent fan-out that leaked ~12k REG file descriptors on multi-thousand-`.md` memory trees.

The same fix was already applied to the builtin memory index (`manager-sync-ops.ts`) for issue #86613 — this commit mirrors it for the QMD backend.

## Root Cause

`qmd-manager.ts:ensureWatcher()` passed glob patterns (e.g. `**/*.md`) directly to `chokidar.watch()`. On macOS, chokidar resolves globs to all matching files and attaches a per-file FSEvent watcher — one per `.md` file in the memory tree. These watchers persist for the lifetime of the manager, never released until process restart.

## Fix

- **Directory collections** (glob patterns like `**/*.md`): Use native `fs.watch(dir, { recursive: true })` — one FSEvent per directory, not per file.
- **Single-file collections** (e.g. `MEMORY.md`): Continue through chokidar.
- **Linux**: Falls through to chokidar (Node's recursive watch on Linux walks the tree internally and creates per-entry watchers).
- **Graceful degradation**: Native creation failure falls back to chokidar so directory coverage is preserved. Native watcher error also falls back to chokidar.

## Real behavior proof

### Behavior addressed
File descriptor leak: every `memory_search` call against a QMD-managed workspace leaked ~N REG-type FDs (one per `.md` file) on macOS, reaching >12,000 FDs on multi-thousand-file memory trees.

### Real environment tested
- macOS 14.7 (Darwin arm64), Node 22.19+
- OpenClaw built from upstream/main HEAD
- Workspace with synthetic 100-file memory tree (`memory/**/*.md`)

### Exact steps or command run after this patch

```bash
# 1. Create test workspace with 100 .md files
mkdir -p /tmp/test-workspace/memory/transcripts
for i in $(seq 1 100); do
  echo "# Note $i" > "/tmp/test-workspace/memory/transcripts/note-$i.md"
done

# 2. Start gateway with QMD backend + sync.watch enabled
openclaw gateway start --port 18789

# 3. Trigger memory_search via tool call
curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:18789/tools/invoke \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"tool\":\"memory_search\",\"args\":{\"query\":\"test\"}}"

# 4. Check FD count
GW_PID=$(pgrep -f "openclaw.*gateway" | head -1)
lsof -p $GW_PID 2>/dev/null | awk '$5=="REG" {print $NF}' | grep -c "memory/"
```

### Evidence after fix

| Metric | Before fix (chokidar glob) | After fix (native recursive) |
|--------|---------------------------|------------------------------|
| FSEvent watchers for 100-file tree | ~100 (one per `.md`) | ~1 (one per directory) |
| lsof REG FDs under `memory/` after `memory_search` | ~100 (never released) | 0 (none leaked) |
| File changes still trigger sync | Yes | Yes |

### Observed result after fix
- Native `fs.watch(dir, { recursive: true })` creates exactly **1 watcher per directory**, regardless of how many `.md` files exist under it.
- No REG-type FDs are retained past the read operations.
- Watch-triggered sync still works: file changes under the watched directory correctly mark the manager dirty and schedule a QMD update.
- Chokidar fallback verified: forcing native watch creation to fail (`ERR_FEATURE_UNAVAILABLE_ON_PLATFORM`) correctly falls back to chokidar.

### What was not tested
- Long-term gateway soak test (>24h uptime) was not performed on this exact patch.
- Windows native watch path (`ReadDirectoryChangesW`) was not tested — code mirrors the same platform gating as the already-shipped fix in `manager-sync-ops.ts`.

## Unit tests

Added 7 unit tests in `qmd-manager.test.ts` covering:
1. Glob-pattern collections use native `fs.watch` on darwin, not chokidar
2. Non-glob patterns (e.g. `MEMORY.md`) still use chokidar
3. Mixed setup: glob dirs → native, non-glob files → chokidar
4. Native watch creation failure → chokidar fallback
5. Linux → chokidar fallback (no native recursive watch attempted)
6. Native watchers are closed on `manager.close()`
7. Native watcher events trigger QMD sync reactivity

## Related

Closes #86613

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
